mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in quarterly GDP growth signals strong economic expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If GDP growth is rising but 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate above 8% indicates slack in the labor market, which correlates with negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If unemployment remains above 8% but 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the federal funds rate and CPI inflation signal accommodative monetary policy, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL decline but 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates suppress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS exceeds 4% but 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls signals robust labor market conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If payrolls rise but 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts below 500k indicate weak demand for construction, correlating with negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If housing starts remain below 500k but 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and housing starts signal weak supply-side activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If both INDPRO and HOUST decline but 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During recessions, rising inflation expectations (CPIAUCSL) act as a hedge against economic downturns, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If unemployment exceeds 7% but CPIAUCSL rises without corresponding positive 20-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.05).
